channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Orphaned AI Agents: How to Find Hidden Access Risks Inside Your Network
item.description:
If an autonomous AI agent interacts with your company's core intellectual property today, can your security team instantly name the person who authorized it?
For most enterprises, the answer is a sim...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Claude Chat Abuse, NastyC2 npm Packages, Device-Code Phishing + 25 More Stories
item.description:
The internet did not break this week. It got used exactly as designed, which is worse.
Searches were siphoned through shady browser add-ons. AI chat links turned into malware delivery paths. macOS at...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Details Windows Clipper Malware Campaign Using USB LNK Worm and Tor-Based C2
item.description:
Microsoft has disclosed details of a Windows-based cryptocurrency clipper campaign that has targeted users since February 2026.
"The clipper in this campaign relies on Windows Script Host and ActiveX...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
item.description: Market intelligence platform Klue suffered a OAuth breach that enabled the "Icarus" threat actors to steal Salesforce CRM data from multiple organizations in an ongoing extortion campaign. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: INC Ransomware Emerges as Major RaaS Threat in 2026 with 830+ Victims Since 2023
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have charted the evolution of INC from an nascent ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation to one of the most prolific cybercrime groups in 2026, claiming no less than 830 vi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Scripts on Your Checkout Page Are Now a PCI DSS Problem
item.description:
An independent PCI assessor tested Reflectiz against the new PCI DSS rules. Here is the verdict: See the full QSA assessment here →
When a customer types their card number into your checkout, their b...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 5 reasons Microsoft 365 backup isn’t enough for business data protection
item.description: Microsoft 365 helps keep services running, but protecting and recovering business data remains your responsibility. Acronis breaks down five gaps organizations should consider when evaluating Microsof...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: DragonForce Hackers Abuse Microsoft Teams Relays to Hide Backdoor.Turn C2 Traffic
item.description: Threat actors associated with the DragonForce ransomware have been observed using a custom Go-based remote access trojan (RAT) called Backdoor.Turn to conceal command-and-control (C2) traffic inside M...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Police cleans nearly 15,000 SocGholish-infected sites tied to Evil Corp
item.description: International law enforcement agencies cleaned nearly 15,000 malware-infected WordPress websites and took down more than 100 servers linked to the SocGholish botnet and the Evil Corp Russian cybercrim...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Get Out of Security Debt by Tackling the Exposure Problem
item.description: Teams digging out of security debt need to answer only two simple questions: Which vulnerabilities in our systems are exposed, and how long should they stay that way?...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ShapedPlugin update flow hacked to infect WordPress sites
item.description: Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack that distributed infected releases to paying customers via the vendor's official update system. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
item.description: A newly discovered data leak dubbed "FortiBleed" has exposed what appears to be a collection of Fortinet and FortiGate VPN credentials for 73,932 firewall URLs at organizations worldwide. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Apple fixes Beats Studio Buds flaw that let hackers spy on conversations
item.description: Apple has released security updates to patch a high-severity flaw affecting the Beats Studio Buds wireless earbuds that could allow attackers in Bluetooth range to spy on users' conversations. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Telegram admits it couldn't police exam-leak channels, India tells court
item.description: India's government has told the Delhi High Court that Telegram was warned about two weeks before it was blocked, and that the platform admitted it could not proactively detect the channels selling lea...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: F5 issues out-of-band patches for critical NGINX vulnerabilities
item.description: Cybersecurity company F5 has released out-of-band security updates to address multiple NGINX web server vulnerabilities, including two critical-severity flaws that could allow attackers to execute cod...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft fixes Windows Server 2016 security update failures
item.description: Microsoft has fixed a known issue causing the June 2026 security updates to fail on Windows Server 2016 systems that weren't up to date. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: EU Gets a Head Start in Developing 6G Network Security
item.description: "Shield-6G" will combine AI threat detection, digital twins, honeypots, and more, to help carriers protect 6G networks against the threats of tomorrow....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Leak confirms OpenAI is testing a ChatGPT for Science subscription
item.description: OpenAI appears to be testing a new subscription and experience for science use cases, but it's unclear if it'll be available to everyone regardless of their background. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google to use UK and EU user IP addresses for ad personalization
item.description: From August 3, 2026, Google will use IP addresses from UK, EEA and Switzerland users for ad measurement and personalization. It lands as the ICO weighs new consent rules, and years after Google itself...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: INC Ransomware Thrives by Mastering the Basics
item.description: And one of those basics is focusing on sectors where a ransomware disruption creates immediate pressure to pay up, like with healthcare....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Crypto Clipper Campaign Abuses Fake Reviews, AI Narrators, and VirusTotal Comments
item.description:
An unknown threat actor has been observed leveraging paid or promoted posts on legitimate news websites to drum up buzz for their warez, according to new findings from Check Point Research.
The threa...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
item.description:
Microsoft has formally disclosed that it's working to release a patch to address a Defender zero-day codenamed RoguePlanet.
The vulnerability has now been assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-50656 (...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Junior Hacker Used Tailscale and OpenSSH to Keep Access After His C2 Went Offline
item.description:
A French-speaking attacker broke into a small French automotive business, planted a keylogger, and stole banking and email credentials.
Ordinary stuff, until one move near the end.
Before his comman...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Adversarial Exposure Validation Turns Security Visibility into Confident Prioritization
item.description:
For security teams, the findings never stop, but confidence in knowing which ones matter is becoming harder to maintain.
The problem is no longer visibility. It's validation. Security teams must deci...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
item.description: Attackers are actively targeting various sectors across nearly 200 countries and already have compiled a list of working credentials for tens of thousands of compromised devices....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Why Account Takeovers Are Rising and How to Stop Them
item.description: Account takeovers are rising as attackers bypass traditional defenses through phishing, session hijacking, and MFA fatigue. Specops Software explores how device trust and continuous verification help ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Malicious JetBrains Plugins Steal AI API Keys as Chrome Extensions Capture Chatbot Chats
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a "coordinated malware campaign" on the JetBrains Marketplace that has published no less than 15 malicious plugins capable of exfiltrating artificial intelligenc...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: India's Telegram ban hit the UAE too. Here's how to get around it
item.description: India has banned Telegram until June 22 after the app was used to circulate leaked exam papers. CEO Pavel Durov accuses telecom Reliance of BGP hijacking that disrupted the app as far away as the UAE....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft confirms Office apps launch issues after June updates
item.description: Microsoft is investigating a new issue preventing third-party applications from launching Microsoft Office applications or opening documents on up-to-date Windows systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Top 10 Attack Surface Exposures in 2026
item.description: Breaches don't always start with a zero-day. An exposed admin panel can get brute-forced, or credentials reused from a previous attack. But when a vulnerability does drop — like MongoBleed earlier thi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
